backlog.title: Security & Correctness Bug Fixes
backlog.description: Fix the 4 issues identified in the end-to-end validation of the §8.1 HTML Document Envelope: 2 Major (shell-quoting failure in plugin bindings, HTML attribute injection via --font) and 2 Minor (render --output missing parent dir creation, --lang empty string locale derivation). All fixes are surgical, grounded in verified codebase locations, and each includes adversarial test coverage for the previously-unexercised shell-quoting and HTML-attribute boundaries.

milestones.title: Fix Major Issues (Shell Quoting + XSS)
milestones.description: Fix the two Major severity issues: (1) apostrophe in @tmux-2html-title silently breaks all prefix bindings due to naive single-quote wrapping in tmux-2html.tmux; (2) HTML attribute injection / stored-XSS via --font / @tmux-2html-font due to unescaped font-family value in the <pre style> attribute. Both are independent fixes in different files.

tasks.title: Shell-escape @tmux-2html-title / @tmux-2html-lang in plugin bindings (Issue 1)
tasks.description: Fix the naive single-quote wrapping of title_opt/lang_opt in tmux-2html.tmux that causes an apostrophe in the value (e.g. Bob's pane) to break /bin/sh parsing of the run-shell command, silently disabling ALL three prefix bindings (O/full, visible, C-o/region). Replace with a POSIX-safe shell_escape function that wraps the value in single quotes and replaces every embedded ' with '\''. Add an adversarial test case to tests/plugin_options.sh that exercises the apostrophe boundary.

subtasks.title: Add POSIX shell_escape function and apply to title_arg/lang_arg with adversarial test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md §Issue 1 and architecture/external_deps.md §POSIX Shell Single-Quote Escaping. The bug is in tmux-2html.tmux (~lines 111-115): `title_arg="--title '$title_opt'"` wraps the value in naive single quotes. An apostrophe in title_opt (e.g. "Bob's pane") produces `--title 'Bob's pane'` which is an unbalanced single-quote string that breaks /bin/sh parsing of the entire run-shell command. All THREE bindings interpolate $title_arg/$lang_arg: O/full (~line 138), visible (~line 145), C-o/region (~line 185). The 2>/dev/null on the pane subprocess swallows the parse error, so the user sees only an empty status message. Note: @tmux-2html-lang is NOT affected in practice because its value is BCP-47-normalized (cannot contain apostrophe), but the same fix should be applied for defense-in-depth.
2. INPUT: The existing tmux-2html.tmux plugin script (POSIX sh, ~240 lines). The existing tests/plugin_options.sh harness which mocks `tmux()` and captures bind-key/display-popup command strings. The read_opt() function already reads @tmux-2html-title and @tmux-2html-lang via show-option. The test harness run_loader() function takes $1=title, $2=lang, $3=visible seeds.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add a shell_escape() shell function in tmux-2html.tmux (in §3, before title_arg construction) that takes a value and outputs it POSIX-safely: wrapped in single quotes with every embedded ' replaced by '\'' . Use the sed-based idiom: `printf "%s" "$1" | sed "s/'/'\\\\''/g"` then wrap in single quotes via printf. (b) Replace `title_arg="--title '$title_opt'"` with `title_arg="--title $(shell_escape "$title_opt")"` (and same for lang_arg). (c) IMPORTANT: The shell_escape output replaces the entire `'$title_opt'` fragment (including the surrounding single quotes) — shell_escape itself adds the wrapping quotes. (d) In tests/plugin_options.sh, add a new test case (c) after the existing (b) case: call run_loader with title="Bob's pane", then for each of the 3 bindings assert: (1) the captured bound command's title fragment parses under `/bin/sh -n` (syntax-only check, no execution); (2) the debug seam shows title_arg containing `--title` followed by the shell-escaped value. Also test lang with an apostrophe for completeness. (e) Run `sh tests/plugin_options.sh` to confirm PASS. (f) Run `scripts/check-safety.sh` to confirm no new FAIL/WARN (the fix adds a sed call, not a PATH shim — safe). DO NOT create any tmux shim or touch real tmux. DO NOT add unbounded >> logs.
4. OUTPUT: Modified tmux-2html.tmux with shell_escape() function applied to title_arg and lang_arg. Modified tests/plugin_options.sh with adversarial apostrophe test case(s). The shell_escape function must be defined BEFORE the title_arg/lang_arg construction lines so it is available at source time.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update docs/CONFIGURATION.md §@tmux-2html-title to note that values containing special characters (including apostrophes) are now safely shell-escaped in the generated bindings. This rides WITH the work, do not create a separate docs subtask.

tasks.title: HTML-escape --font / @tmux-2html-font to prevent attribute injection (Issue 2)
tasks.description: Fix the stored-XSS vector where a user-supplied font family value (via --font CLI flag or @tmux-2html-font tmux option) is emitted unescaped into the <pre style="font-family: ..."> attribute in ghostty_format.zig. A double-quote in the value breaks out of the attribute and injects arbitrary HTML attributes (including DOM-event handlers). HTML-escape the font value at the emission point so it cannot break out of the style attribute.

subtasks.title: Escape font-family value at emission point in ghostty_format.zig with unit test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md §Issue 2 and architecture/external_deps.md §HTML Attribute Escaping. The bug is at src/ghostty_format.zig lines 840-841: `const font = self.opts.font orelse "monospace"; buf_writer.print("font-family: {s};", .{font})` — the {s} format interpolates the font value RAW into the double-quoted style attribute. A `"` in the value breaks out. The existing writeEscaped function (render.zig:299) escapes & < > \" ' — the same set needed here. The buf_writer is a ghostty stream writer with .writeAll() and .writeByte() methods. Golden tests (golden_test.zig:30) use null font → "monospace" (no special chars), so goldens will NOT change. The font flows from cli.zig opts.font → renderGrid(font) → ScreenFormatter.init(.font=font) → self.opts.font → unescaped emit. All three subcommands (render/pane/region) share this path.
2. INPUT: The existing src/ghostty_format.zig file (absorbed Ghostty ScreenFormatter, ~1557+ lines). The <pre> wrapper code at lines 816-842 is project-specific (not upstream Ghostty). The buf_writer variable at line 831 is `stream.writer()` with .print(), .writeAll(), .writeByte() methods. The opts.font field (?[]const u8) at line 133. The existing writeEscaped function at render.zig:299 (for reference on the escape set, but it writes to std.Io.Writer — not directly usable here since buf_writer is a different type).
3. LOGIC: (a) In src/ghostty_format.zig, replace line 841 `buf_writer.print("font-family: {s};", .{font})` with an inline HTML-escape loop. Write `font-family: ` prefix, then iterate each byte of font: for '"' write `"`, for '&' write `&`, for '<' write `<`, for '>' write `>`, for '\'' write `'`, else write the byte itself. Then write the trailing `;`. All writes use `catch return error.WriteFailed` (matching the existing error handling pattern at lines 831, 836, 842). (b) DO NOT change the default value ("monospace" stays unescaped — it contains no special chars). (c) Add a unit test in src/ghostty_format.zig OR src/render.zig (wherever formatter tests live) that calls renderGrid (or the formatter directly) with font = `a" onmouseover="alert(1)` and asserts: (1) the output HTML contains `font-family: a" onmouseover="alert(1);` (or equivalent entity-escaped form); (2) the output does NOT contain a raw `"` that would break out of the style attribute; (3) no extra HTML attribute (like `onmouseover=`) appears outside the style value. If placing the test in render.zig, follow the pattern of the existing `test "writeEscaped: ..."` at line 1338 (uses std.Io.Writer.Allocating). (d) Run `zig build test --release=fast` to confirm all tests pass (including the new one and existing goldens).
4. OUTPUT: Modified src/ghostty_format.zig with HTML-escaped font-family emission. New unit test proving the XSS vector is closed. No golden test changes needed (goldens use null font).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update docs/CONFIGURATION.md §@tmux-2html-font to note that the font value is now HTML-escaped in the output (security improvement, no behavior change for normal font names). This rides WITH the work, do not create a separate docs subtask.

milestones.title: Fix Minor Issues & Sync Documentation
milestones.description: Fix the two Minor severity issues: (3) render --output does not create parent directories (inconsistency with pane/region); (4) --lang explicit empty string yields 'en' rather than deriving from locale. Then sync all changeset-level documentation (README.md, docs/CONFIGURATION.md) to reflect the security and behavior improvements from both milestones.

tasks.title: Create parent directories in render --output path (Issue 3)
tasks.description: Fix the inconsistency where pane and region call makePath before writing output files, but the render subcommand's renderToFileAtomic and writeDocFileAtomic functions do not — causing `render --output nested/deep/out.html` to fail with 'cannot write output file' when nested/deep/ does not exist.

subtasks.title: Add makePath in renderToFileAtomic and writeDocFileAtomic with test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md §Issue 3. The bug is in src/render.zig: renderToFileAtomic (line 538) and writeDocFileAtomic (line 602) both compute dir_path = std.fs.path.dirname(path) orelse "." then call openDir(dir_path) directly WITHOUT calling makePath first. If dir_path doesn't exist, openDir fails → error → 'cannot write output file'. Pane already does it right at main.zig:496: `std.fs.cwd().makePath(out_dir) catch {};`. Region does it right at region.zig:484: `if (std.fs.path.dirname(path)) |dp| std.fs.cwd().makePath(dp) catch {};`. The fix mirrors region's pattern (dirname may be null for bare filenames).
2. INPUT: The existing src/render.zig file. renderToFileAtomic at line 538-575: takes (alloc, path, ansi, size, colors, font, doc), computes dir_path at line 540, opens dir at lines 551-554. writeDocFileAtomic at line 602-635: takes (alloc, path, doc, fragment_bytes), computes dir_path, opens dir. Both functions already compute `const dir_path = std.fs.path.dirname(path) orelse ".";` and have the dir handle opening logic right after.
3. LOGIC: (a) In renderToFileAtomic (after line 540 where dir_path is computed, before line 551 where dir is opened), add: `std.fs.cwd().makePath(dir_path) catch {};` — idempotent, ignores errors (the subsequent openDir will report the real failure). (b) In writeDocFileAtomic (after its dir_path computation, before its dir opening), add the same line. (c) Add a unit test in src/render.zig that: creates a temp dir, constructs a nested output path like `<tmp>/nested/deep/out.html` where nested/deep/ does NOT exist, calls renderToFileAtomic (or writeDocFileAtomic) with minimal valid args, asserts the file was created and contains valid HTML. Clean up the temp dir afterward. Follow the existing test patterns in render.zig (the file has many test functions). (d) Run `zig build test --release=fast` to confirm. NOTE: For the test, use std.testing.allocator and a real temp dir (std.posix.getenv("TMPDIR") or fallback). DO NOT write large scratch to /tmp (put it under a repo-local scratch/ dir or use the test's own managed temp).
4. OUTPUT: Modified src/render.zig with makePath calls in both renderToFileAtomic and writeDocFileAtomic. New unit test proving nested output paths work. The render --output path is now consistent with pane and region.
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change (this fixes a bug to match documented behavior; --output already implies the file is written). If the render --help text mentions parent dirs, update it, but current help text does not.

tasks.title: Treat --lang empty string as locale-derived (Issue 4)
tasks.description: Fix the behavior where `--lang ""` (explicit empty string) yields 'en' instead of deriving from the locale. The CLI parser sets opts.lang to Some("") for --lang "", which resolveLang treats as an explicit value, calls toBcp47("") which returns null (length < 2), and falls back to 'en'. An explicit empty should mean 'derive from locale' (same as omitting the flag), consistent with how the plugin omits --lang when the option is unset.

subtasks.title: Modify resolveLang to treat empty explicit as locale-derived with unit test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md §Issue 4 and architecture/external_deps.md §Zig optional string handling. The bug is in src/render.zig lines 292-294: `pub fn resolveLang(explicit: ?[]const u8) []const u8 { if (explicit) |e| return toBcp47(e) orelse "en"; return langFromEnv(); }`. When --lang "" is passed, cli.zig sets opts.lang = @as(?[]const u8, "") (non-null, empty slice). resolveLang unwraps it to e="", calls toBcp47("") which returns null (lang_src.len < 2), and falls back to "en" — skipping locale derivation. The fix: treat empty explicit the same as null/unset. langFromEnv() at line 286 reads LC_ALL > LC_MESSAGES > LANG > "en".
2. INPUT: The existing src/render.zig file. resolveLang at line 292-294. toBcp47 at line 221. langFromEnv at line 286 (calls langFromEnvStrings at line 269). The existing unit tests for toBcp47 at lines 1479-1497 and writeEscaped at line 1338 (pattern reference for test structure).
3. LOGIC: (a) In resolveLang (render.zig:292), add an empty-string guard before the toBcp47 call: if explicit is non-null but the unwrapped value has length 0, fall through to langFromEnv(). The modified function: `pub fn resolveLang(explicit: ?[]const u8) []const u8 { if (explicit) |e| { if (e.len == 0) return langFromEnv(); return toBcp47(e) orelse "en"; } return langFromEnv(); }`. (b) Add a unit test in src/render.zig: set up environment with LANG=de_DE.UTF-8 (and LC_ALL/LC_MESSAGES unset), call resolveLang(@as(?[]const u8, "")), assert it returns "de-DE" (locale-derived, not "en"). NOTE: langFromEnv reads the process env directly via std.posix.getenv, so the test may need to set the env var — check if existing tests mock env or if resolveLang can be tested via langFromEnvStrings instead. If env mocking is impractical in unit tests, test langFromEnvStrings("", "", "de_DE.UTF-8") returns "de-DE" (the underlying pure function that resolveLang delegates to). (c) Also verify the existing resolveLang behavior for non-empty invalid (e.g. resolveLang("C") still returns "en") is unchanged. (d) Run `zig build test --release=fast` to confirm.
4. OUTPUT: Modified src/render.zig with resolveLang treating empty explicit as locale-derived. New unit test proving locale derivation works for empty explicit. The non-empty behavior (explicit invalid → "en") is unchanged.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update docs/CONFIGURATION.md §@tmux-2html-lang / --lang to clarify that an empty explicit value (--lang "") is treated as unset and falls back to locale derivation. This rides WITH the work, do not create a separate docs subtask.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Final documentation sweep to ensure README.md and docs/CONFIGURATION.md reflect all security and behavior improvements from this changeset. Per §5 Mode B, this is the catch-all for cross-cutting docs that only make sense once the whole changeset is in place.

subtasks.title: Update README.md and docs/CONFIGURATION.md with changeset summary
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: This is the §5 Mode B changeset-level documentation task. It depends on ALL implementing subtasks (P1.M1.T1.S1, P1.M1.T2.S1, P1.M2.T1.S1, P1.M2.T2.S1) because it summarizes the entire delta. Per-subtask doc updates (Mode A) were already done by each implementing subtask; this task handles cross-cutting/overview docs only.
2. INPUT: The completed changes from all 4 implementing subtasks. The existing README.md (project overview, features, usage). The existing docs/CONFIGURATION.md (all @tmux-2html-* options, --font, --title, --lang). The PRD Testing Summary (h2.4) which documents the two vectors that standard validation was blind to.
3. LOGIC: (a) Review README.md for any feature/security claims that should be updated: if README mentions the HTML output is safe to share (§8.1 trust guarantee), ensure it reflects that --font is now HTML-escaped. If README has a security or trust section, update it. If README lists features or known issues, remove any that are now fixed. (b) Review docs/CONFIGURATION.md for consistency: ensure @tmux-2html-title documents the shell-escaping fix (from P1.M1.T1.S1 Mode A), @tmux-2html-font documents the HTML-escaping fix (from P1.M1.T2.S1 Mode A), @tmux-2html-lang documents the empty-string locale-derivation behavior (from P1.M2.T2.S1 Mode A). If any Mode A updates were missed by the implementing subtasks, fill them in here. (c) Do NOT create new doc files unless the changeset genuinely introduces a new concept requiring its own page. Prefer updating existing files. (d) Run `scripts/check-safety.sh` and `scripts/preflight.sh` as a final safety check after all edits.
4. OUTPUT: Updated README.md and docs/CONFIGURATION.md reflecting the complete changeset. No stale claims about behavior that has changed.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation task — no further doc updates needed.
